title: Seviyan Vermicelli

seo.title: How to Make Seviyan Vermicelli — Easy Pakistani Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Seviyan Vermicelli. This easy Pakistani recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Seviyan Vermicelli — a beloved Pakistani classic

description: Seviyan Vermicelli is a cherished Pakistani sweet dish, often gracing tables during Eid and other festive celebrations. This comforting dessert features delicate roasted vermicelli noodles simmered in rich milk, sweetened with sugar and infused with aromatic cardamom. It offers a wonderfully creamy texture and a delightful blend of flavors, making it a truly satisfying treat.

instructions.title: Roast the Vermicelli
instructions.text: Heat ghee or unsalted butter in a heavy-bottomed pot or large saucepan over medium-low heat. Add the broken vermicelli pieces and roast them, stirring continuously, until they turn golden brown and fragrant. This usually takes about 5-7 minutes. Be careful not to burn them. Transfer the roasted vermicelli to a plate and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Milk Base
instructions.text: In the same pot, add the full-fat milk and the lightly crushed green cardamom pods. Bring the milk to a gentle boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally to prevent it from sticking to the bottom. Once it boils, reduce the heat to low.

instructions.title: Sweeten the Milk
instructions.text: Add the granulated sugar to the simmering milk. Stir well until the sugar completely dissolves. You can adjust the amount of sugar according to your preference for sweetness. Allow the milk to simmer for another 2-3 minutes to infuse the cardamom flavor.

instructions.title: Add Vermicelli and Cook
instructions.text: Carefully add the roasted vermicelli back into the pot with the sweetened milk. Stir gently to ensure the vermicelli is fully submerged. Increase the heat slightly to bring it to a very gentle simmer again, then reduce heat to low.

instructions.title: Simmer Until Tender
instructions.text: Cover the pot and let the seviyan simmer on very low heat for about 10-15 minutes, or until the vermicelli becomes soft and tender, and the milk has thickened slightly. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking and ensure even cooking. If adding raisins, stir them in during the last 5 minutes of cooking.

instructions.title: Finish and Garnish
instructions.text: Once the vermicelli is cooked and the seviyan has reached your desired consistency, remove the pot from the heat. Stir in the kewra essence if using. Garnish generously with sliced almonds and pistachios before serving. You can serve it warm or chilled.

tips: Always roast the vermicelli until golden brown. This step is crucial for developing a nutty flavor and preventing the seviyan from becoming mushy.
For an extra rich and creamy texture, you can add a tablespoon of condensed milk or a pinch of saffron threads soaked in warm milk along with the sugar.
Seviyan Vermicelli can be served warm, at room temperature, or chilled. It tends to thicken as it cools, so if you prefer a thinner consistency, add a splash of warm milk before serving.
article: Seviyan Vermicelli is a beloved Pakistani Pasta and Noodles dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
